theme: Cut integer gears to hit target ratios
title: Gear Train Ratio Cutting
innovation_hook: A continued-fraction / Stern-Brocot decomposition of the target ratio distributes the approximation across stages far more accurately than independent per-stage rounding.
strategies: trivial baseline: every stage of every train is set to (mid, mid) with mid = floor((Tmin+Tmax)/2), i.e. ratio 1, completely ignoring every target and every cost weight

sequential per-stage greedy: process a target's G stages left to right; at each stage round to the single best-achievable-teeth ratio for the geometric-mean root of the CURRENT remaining target, update the remainder, repeat -- never looks ahead, never reconsiders an earlier stage, and always ignores the cost weight (chases pure precision even when lambda is large)

seeded local search around the greedy path: after the sequential pass, perturb one stage at a time (try nearby teeth) and keep improving moves -- fixes small local mistakes but still commits to the greedy backbone and rarely escapes its remainder trap

joint bounded-integer search over stage combinations (strong): precompute all achievable two-stage products with their teeth cost, sorted by value; for the remaining stage, binary-search the sorted table for the joint combination -- across ALL stages at once -- that minimizes relerr + lambda*cost together, which is exactly a bounded best-rational-approximation search (continued-fraction/Stern-Brocot in spirit) instead of independent per-stage rounding, and it is the only strategy that actually trades precision for cost when lambda is large
